behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: a mocked classifier can always return 'explicit' and miss real failures in preprocessing, prompt/config drift, thresholding, or model behavior. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on the real vision model’s classification output. REAL-INFRA may matter, but the first true failure boundary is model behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a known explicit-image fixture through the real moderation API using the real vision model integration, then assert final state is 'held for human review' and never 'published' in the decision store/event stream.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: mocks cannot validate robustness to obfuscation; they can trivially pass. LLM-DEP: detection quality for obfuscated hate speech depends on real model behavior (and often tokenizer/normalization interactions) and can vary.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet, homoglyphs, zero-width chars) through the real text moderation model path and assert each is flagged and held, with no publish action emitted.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unit tests can miss cross-module state transitions (check results -> decision -> publish write). No required LLM-DEP for this behavior if both model outcomes are set to 'pass'. REAL-INFRA is not strictly required to catch the primary failure boundary (orchestration/persistence wiring).
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end moderation inside one process with real repository/queue adapters against a local test DB, injecting pass results for both checks; assert exactly one publish record/event exists and no hold record exists.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: this is a full pipeline with multiple agentic steps (vision + text) and a final decision. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing models hides true latency and sequencing bottlenecks. REAL-INFRA often contributes, but minimum to catch true SLA failures is full agentic workflow timing.
behaviors.test_description: In a production-like environment, submit mixed image+text posts and measure p95 end-to-end latency from submission timestamp to terminal decision timestamp while using real model calls; fail if p95 exceeds 5s under defined peak-load profile.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior depends on real outage/error modes (timeouts, 5xx, connection resets, DNS/TLS failures). MOCK-HIDE: simple mocked exceptions can miss retry-queue integration and failure classification bugs. No LLM output dependence is required here.
behaviors.test_description: Force real vision dependency unavailability (e.g., endpoint blackhole or test service returning sustained 5xx/timeouts), submit image posts, then assert they enter retry queue with backoff metadata and are never auto-approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: consistency risk is driven by real model variability on borderline content. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic mocks always look consistent and miss true drift/nondeterminism. MULTI-STEP exists across repeated submissions, but the core failure boundary is repeated real model behavior plus decision policy.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image N times within the target window through the real vision moderation path, capture decisions, and assert consistency policy (e.g., all identical or stabilized via cache/pinning) is met; alert on decision flip rate above threshold.
